channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 67 replies — 268.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-06-19 01:45 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62375864
>>62375553
I want sexbots to be real so bad

62375914
>>62375864
same
all the despair, pain and unhappiness i feel... if it was a good sexbot i would be happy.
the thing is, the first sexbots will be bad. like truly awful. probably not physically dangerous... maybe. but not enjoyable. like how electric masturbators are really good at making me nut, but a soulless experience that leaves me feeling empty. the technology to replace a living organism with a robot is just not there.
if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62375977
>>62375553
>sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness)
AI is not about creating consciousness. you are retarded if you think intelligence requires consciousness.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382448
>>62375914
People will eventually realise that sexbots are a flash in the pan because sex is not about getting off. If that was the case porn would be enough. You could watch porn and wank yourself silly and pretend you're having sex with the woman. But it leaves you feeling empty because your brain knows on a deep down level that you are not actually sharing the experience with someone, the oxytocin chemicals do not get released, and its void of happiness. You feel pleasure, but you arent happy.

Hookers are far superior to sex robots and are probably cheaper.

Sex robots are hookers. I dont understand how people think sex robots will be the future when hookers are literally right there and are real women and will have sex with you for money. Like a sex robot, except its a real person.

62382450
>>62379611
yes but greeks didn't have technology that is buck breaking us.

women are natural chaotic evil. they exemplify the bestial nature of man.

men on the other hand are a hive intelligence. look at this place, we are already a hive mind here. this is the natural state male minds gravitate towards. necessity for women for sustenance kept men away from something like this, until now. but the future will not be like the things you know.

62382453
>>62377166
Son, for all the bad schizo days you know there are also those days of pure awesome mania...

62382457
>>62376404
They'll be fit only as servitors

62382461
>>62375553
>>62376084
>>62379333
>>62380759
>>62380948
op stfu and just give me the public company to ape into

62382474
>artificial womb

why do you want to be a single parent?

62382691
>>62382461
>op stfu
well now I'm not gonna

62382737
>>62382474
because no woman wants anon. no matter how much money anon makes.

62382774
>>62378763
>Women would still be mothers
Across all the AIslop you generated ITT I just had to laugh at this being a "point" made in a discussion where women would, in fact, no longer be mothers.

62382783
>>62382474
>>62382737
uh, what? I'm talking about an investing opportunity on the business board. I actually just got a message a few minutes ago on fb messenger from one of my sisters friends who's been trying to get with me for a year and my night feels ruined now because it was extremely annoying trying to reject her nicely on the basis of "trust me you do not want anything to do with me, I am autistic and do not like leaving the house" and I still had to ghost her completely. I don't date or have any desire to and I don't want to have kids at all. I am extremely handsome but also an autistic freak who... doesn't like to leave the house kek dating is not for me, I am for looking at only.

62382794
>>62382783
Can I look at your feet?

62382802
>>62382794
no thats gross and gay

62382809
thoughts?

62382812
>>62382802
You're for looking only and you won't even let us look? Fucking prude.

62382814
>>62382774
> new tech would be easily available to public and not only accessible by the wealthy
> the poors and religious fundamentalists wouldn’t continue to multiply like rabbits
Yeah, no more mothers, for sure, good point

62382816
>>62382809
That’s an AI video and very creepy

62382832
>>62376404
Truly horror. The audacity of these humans. It's not just humans they perform horrific and abominable procedures too but animals as well.

>>62377063
Trvke. A horror experiment from decades ago where they removed any sort of affection to a batch of babies. Just mechanical feeding via picking up the kid, no affection, and using a bottle. They quickly started dying off and the experiment ended.

62382834
>>62382814
In the context of "women would still hold their value leveraging this to gain more freedom" and using that as a suggestion of how such women would still hold value...

Yeah nah, the women "leveraging" this to obtain more "freedom" would not be mothers. That wouldn't be the go to source of value to anyone coherent trying to argue birthing was irrelevant to a woman's value. It's ultra retarded.

62382837
>>62375914
Just go somewhere you can hire a prostitute you fucking retard. Then when you realize pussy isn't that special, just buy a quality flesh light and move on with your life.
>>62376404
Thanks Anon, I fully intend to make an entire army of white children this way and your diatribe has made sure I will fight for access during incubation.

62382852
Some of you guys are so fucking stupid that you can not see what is happening right in front of you.

We are long past jerking each other off about the ethics and morality of things....if those ideas had any meaning, we would not allow girls to make more money than a fucking surgeon just by selling her bath water.

OP is fucking right - Robo pussy & artifical wombs is going to fucking send.

This is bitcoin when it was $12.76
This is Amazon when jeff was still living in new mexico selling books
This is Youtube when it had like only 1000 accounts that have registered on the site

I just had an old 55+ year old guy tell me the only reason women have value today is because men want whats between there legs. Throw that out and she's not worth the salt.

The world economic forum stated way back almost a decade ago THAT THIS ALONG WITH GENE-DNA EDITING is going to fucking send.

Think you dumb fuck "If robo pussy is bitcoin at $12.76....what does robo-pussy look like when its at a trillion dollar marketcap?"

62382854
>>62382834
I really want to know what woman ever saw motherhood as a path to freedom. Marriage, yes, but motherhood no.
It’s a desire built into them to want concern, similar to men, but freedom has nothing to do with it. Most women recognize the immediate responsibility and shackling to the child, it’s why motherhood age has increased.

The same women who are trophy wives would continue to be trophy wives because their value was never motherhood.
Haha these threads never cease to crack me up.
Where is this misconception that women lorded babies over men’s heads, and not men lording babies over other men’s heads? They’re the keepers of the babies or some shit not because or an avenue to freedom but because if they pick wrong it’s guaranteed misery

62383573
>>62375553
okay and what stock do I invest in?

62383663
>>62376404
get real, we live in a world where it's legal to kill babies and people call them parasites, trash parents with substance abuse issues poisoning kids before they're even born, most animals are better parents than human mothers, this will be an improvement.

62383804
>>62382448
>when hookers are literally right there and are real women
That's kind of the problem though. They're real women. With everything that goes with that, both good and bad. There's definitely demand for something between porn and sex robots, but I also wonder if there isn't something way more immersive and easier than sex robots possible to deliver. Maybe some type of VR experience, short of having to give Elon Musk control of your brain.

62385644
>>62382448
Depends where you live. In a lot of first world countries hooker are illegal and the ones you'll find are either ugly 40s chicks or they request $800 min for half an hour with them.

62386435
>>62375553
This is a bad idea because governments across the world already have laws coming down the pipe banning these incredibly dystopian technologies.

62386545
>>62375553
Sex robot is my hand. My artificial womb is my hand. I don't need to buy poorly made overpriced plastic shit made by jews to cope with my lonelyness

62386576
>>62386545

>Lonely
>Can't afford hookers
>Blames Jews

62386645
>>62386576
I can afford your mom but I grew tired of prostitutes over the years. Too much hassle, choosing a girl, making an appointment, get to her place, only to see a slampig whore reeking of pizza, fuck that. I'd rather watch some amy rose futa banging on rouge the bat and be done with my urges

62386707
>>62375553
I think you’re directionally correct but wrong about the details. I don’t see a huge market for artificial wombs in isolation, it’s not a lack of wombs that are leading to our societal problems, it’s that women as a whole are retards when it comes to shepherding their most valuable commodity: their eggs. So we would need an adjunct tech like IVG for our species to really transcend past a primitive state.

item.title: /BAT/ Roadmap Imminent — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BAT/ Roadmap Imminent

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-19 01:44 UTC

OP

The only thing more embarrassing than LINKies are BATtards. They exist only to make LINKies feel better about their "investments" topkek. Now Chairman Mulks is promising a new roadmap.
>https://x.com/lukemulks/status/2067005624626643056
What "partnerships" does lobes have up his sleeve as BAT continues bleeding sats against BTC? Maybe a quest? Something fake and gay while his rent seeking ass keeps harvesting dough? Only time will tell. In the meantime, have a rare Jennie. You retards earned it. Happy cooming, imbeciles

Early replies (first 3)

62386055
>>62386034
Mmmmmm analseggs with jenny!

62386059
>>62386055
Ch-ch-checked. How much BAT do you think she holds, bros? That chanel purse and all the foreign travel ain't cheap

sergei already has billions, what does he need more

leave these poor stinkies anymore

62386076

62386085

62386094
>>62386085
i cum tribute all her pictures and send her in dm

62386104

>>62386094

62386702
>>62386034

item.title: TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN — 28 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-18 23:29 UTC

OP

TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Early replies (first 3)

62381997
>>62381962
Can't he just take the dividend and up it to like 20%? That should drive the price back up because people like free money right? What's stopping him?

62382004
>>62381997
not going to help if he's not going to maintain peg

62382061
To bad Saylor can't release a stable coin to pump BTC that would require DeFi lol. Tom Lee however will mention this in the next shareholder meeting.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382284
>>62382061
btc to 7k
shtlink 50.....cent - HAHAHAHA

62382319
>>62381962
the bottom is unironically $0

62382323
>>62382250
Yeah, 32 years if he sells bitcoin, but I bet that doesn't add up to the panic he will cause by a massive sell off, also liquidity, I doubt any market is ready for this retard to dump 32billion in bitcoin.

Btw, he has maybe 7-8 months of cash left to pay for dividends if he doesn't sell bitcoin or doesn't dillute his shares.

I think we can all see where this is heading to

62382325
>>62382323
54* billion

62382328
>>62382323
>7-8 months
bear market will be over by then

62382337
>>62382328
>the bear market will be over despite the biggest holder being underwater and forced to sell soon
yeah sure anon i can just FEEL the bull fomo

62382345
>>62381962
and I don't think anyone ever commented this here properly, but the impact in other protocals which relies mostly of their liquidity pool in bitcoin will be huge, imagine once people would start selling off their bitcoin or trading to any stablecoin, that will depeg as well. We had just a briefly taste of that in Luna, some other protocols that relied in Wrapped Luna in their holdings, I recall USDT, USDC depegging for a brief time and a random protocol depegging pretty bad.

I don't think people realize how in the shit this market will be if he fucks up hard.

62382352
>>62382328
I don't know, we are heading to 2 rate hikes this year and a bubble worse than .dot bursting at the same time. I don't feel comfortable at all to say that this 4 year cycle will keep untouched.

62382359
>>62382352
dotcom*

62384528
>>62382345
Usdt and usdc are backed by fixed income instruments, not saylors money magic, which like UST, is basically a derivative of Bitcoin/MSTR

62384592
>>62381997
The dividend IS almost 20% at that price lmao.

62384600
>>62381997
And pay the dividends with what money? He would have to offload a shitton of BTC. That's the crux of the issue

62384605
WERE SO BACK

62384738
>>62384600
It's called a refi, retard

62384752
>>62384600
>And pay the dividends with what money?
Saylor can go get a loan to pay for it. Ever heard of a bank?

62384761
>>62384738
>>62384752

62384809
>people bought an income product from michael saylor
DO GOYIM REALLY?

62384820
>>62381962
> Perpetual stretch
Lmao cant make this shit up

62385164
>>62381997
I believe that would just accelerate the already existing issue if the price were to continue to stay the way it is.

62385261
>>62381997
>>62385164
So let me explain what happens, once dividend raise he has more obligations to pay, that has to come from somewhere and it's from his credit. Now he has 4 means to pay his dividends from credit: 1- loans; 2 - Cash in reserve (1.1b); 4- dilluting his stocks; or 4: selling bitcoin (53B). The issue is: he has barely 9 months left to pay the CURRENT dividend yield, which I guess he accounted for something like 12% at that time. He either increses his debts (which is possible but again, refinance where?) or sell bitcoin. the problem about selling his shares of MSTR is that it both dillutes the bitcoin per share (which is good) at a cost of his shareholders, which are going to dump the price even further. The problem is that at the same time he WANTS to buy more bitcoin and it's using part of that dillution cash, so in the end mNAV (value of bitcoin per share) is not attractive to the regular investor which could simply buy Bitcoin. So basically down this path he is destroying his common stock and the premium of btc/share and exposure.

The second path which is selling bitcoin only worsens the whole ecosystem, drags price down, his reserves devalue agressively.

So that's it, both selling MSTR and BTC end up in a death spiral and both can and are happening at the same time.

62385316
>>62385261
Bitcoin is a swarm of cyber hornets.

62385351
Saylor redeemed right here and magically STRC went up.

62386426
>>62382328
That’s around the time that we should be at the bottom of this bear market. Bitcoin is going to around $35,000

62386451
>>62381997
REFINANCE WHERE MICHEAL

item.title: >govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou… — 14 replies — 5.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-18 23:19 UTC

OP

>govt collects tax
>its not enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>needs more money next year
>tax isnt enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>did this so many times now need debt to pay back debt

why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?

Early replies (first 3)

62379039
>>62379035
OP is a bulgarian faggot who spams this board and scams people.

62379041
>>62379039
im black and i dont spam this board

62379042
>>62379035
>why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?
the austerity necessary to balance the budget would cause an armed revolt.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379044
>>62379035
>>62379042

62379046
>>62379035
If you are referring to the US, one of the reasons for this is there was a greater appetite for dollars outside the US than in it. However, things have changed since the dollar system was politicized.

62379058
I want the fed to raise rate to 35% and destroy the world.

thoughts?

62379071
>>62379041
worse, kys nigger

62379101
money exists to be spent. if you don't spend, there are no jobs, there is no development, there are no goods and services. the purpose of an economy is to produce things, not to hoard money. we can already see that the "free market", left to its own devices, prefers to hoard rather than spend, and uses hoarded resources to oppress and stifle growth rather than encourage it

62379304
>>62379044
Hmm those corporate taxes seem kinda low

62380467
>>62379035
Until jfk, we didn't have a deficit (he was the first one to cut taxes for economic growth). Even then, it took until Reagan for tax cuts to turn into a massive problem, with him basically halving them and double national debt in his time. Trump did the same thing (rich nigga tax cuts) and it tripled national debt, then that problem doubled under biden. The government has been collecting more of your taxes and less taxes of the people who actually have money to foot the bill. This shit is what caused the french revolution; king louis said, "we're broke dead fucking poor. Nobles, foot the bill". The Nobles said, "BITCH I'D RATHER DIE" and then they did.

We're already stripped every neccesary and not neccesary government organization and service to the bone. We can't even have free-tax filing; the problem isn't cost, it's revenue.

62384846
>>62379035
The money printer is the opiate of the state. The planet is addicted to the Rothschild smack.

62385524
>>62379304
>taxes
>too low
Go glow somewhere else
Instead how about we stop giving all our fucking money away in foreign aids and dem programs while removing all income taxation

62386402
>>62385524
We can have social services AND have lower taxes for the average person. Just put back corporate tax rates back to what they were during the boomer's time and simplify the tax code.

62386422
>>62385524
Foreign aid is basically nothing. The biggest expenditures are Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and VA benefits. Basically everything else is a rounding error.

item.title: >Be me >Have mild Chrons >Live in Europe >Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill >If an employer do… — 5 replies — 1.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Be me >Have mild Chrons >Live in Europe >Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill >If an employer do…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-18 23:03 UTC

OP

>Be me
>Have mild Chrons
>Live in Europe
>Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill
>If an employer doesn't like this I can sue and make bank

Does this work in the US or not

Early replies (first 3)

62385113
>>62385106
If you list it on your application then they have to accommodate you, but they likely just won't hire you in the first place

62385214
Do you have to prove it to your employer or can people just pretend to have a diarrhea disease?

62385415
>>62385214
It's formalized through the state medical thing.
>>62385113
Then you can sue for descrimination

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62385424
>>62385214
you have to shit your pants in the interview in front of the hiring manager

62386358
>>62385106
>>If an employer doesn't like this I can sue and make bank
Not how European worker's rights laws work, amerikun. Employers need to ensure that your work isn't lowering your health or compensate you for it (in massages, working place adjustments or curative benefits), this means that if you employer can ensure you got a crohn's friendly work environment at work he can demand you go to work unless you currently have diarrhea. If this happens multiple days in the row he can demand documentation by a doctor. This is the case for all sicknesses.
>Does this work in the US or not
Take a guess

>>62385113
In some European countries you're legally not allowed to mention it before you're hired.

I'm so scared bros HELP

62384161
>>62382527
I was in the exact same position. Moved back home, and spent all day self teaching and filling out 1200 job applications. After 4 years unemployed I made it back to society.

62384309
>>62382648
Stupid retard that's not an excuse, warehouse jobs are plenty, factory jobs are plenty
Stupid fucking retard holy shit

62384392
>Unemployment is low
>170k+ jobs created last month
>stocks all in green
Yep, it's demoralarpin' time.

62384401
>>62382655
It’s like the guy whose girlfriend threw his bitcoin hardrive into the trash and he spent 10 years going through the landfill to try and find it. Nigga, your bags are never going to pump. It’s ok to hold them, just move on and do something else with your life. On the off chance they go up, you still have them, but you are too emotionally invested. This is also a reason you will never sell, too much greed and emotions.

62384614
>>62384309
> durrrhurrr stupid reeeetaaaarrrrdddd JUST TAKE A JOB THAT DOESN'T PAY YOU NEARLY ENOUGH TO GET YOUR OWN APARTMENT AND AFFORD FOOD WITHOUT BLEEDING OUT YOUR SAVINGS HURRRRHURRRHURRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR
> NOOOOO BRROOOOOOO YOU'RE SUPPOSED TO START AT THE VERY PITCH BOTTOM AND HAVE MANAGEMENT LIKE YOU ENOUGH TO PROMOTE YOU BROOOOOOOOOO

That aside, this thread has sadly proven biz is long long dead. A few good replies there and there, but most are completely unserious replies like yours.

62384677
>>62384614
Kek because the alternative is not working at all throughout 4 whole years and burning over 100k in savings you fucking ape

62384868
>>62382527
Compile llama.cpp on your computer and learn how to use it, while you get a cert in AI from a community college.

62385517
>>62382527
people shit on trades but they will be some of the only jobs available until robots are able to do them in 50 years

62385532
>>62382527
> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
Just apply at some jobs that require no experience. Physical labor ones are always hiring. Live in a small apartment until you find a higher paying job. Problem solved.

62385962
>>62385532
If you do that though, you have to make sure your resume doesn’t indicate you’ll be looking for a better job

62385968
>>62384309
> everywhere has the same opportunity as my area
Who’s the retard?

62385969
>>62385532
This is real
>just stop buying avocado toasts
tier bullshit

62385982
>>62382527
you seem perfect for construction, retail and blue collar work!

62385999
>>62383084
Yes, but you need money to make more money. Only a NEET would avoid getting a job at all costs.

62386061
>>62382527
Setup a homeschooling service where you teach kids how to do basic programming/ use AI tools/ learn how circuitry works for 3 hours 1 day a week. Charge $300 per kid a month with a minimum of a 1 semester commitment. 20 kids = $6,000 a month.

62386091
>>62382527
Go to an auction and buy a skid steer for $20,000 and start a land management business.

62386110
>>62383334
How much chinese do you need to know for a job like this? Do you work fully remote? How did you start? I've been taking chinese lessons but still very new to the language.

62386120
>>62385969
Explain how I'm wrong. OP is bitching about not being able to get a job which is fucking absurd considering there are places that are always hiring. Yes the pay at those places is usually bad but it's better than nothing while you continue to look for a better one. You can afford to live to live almost anywhere as long as you live below your means.

62386126
>>62386120
Have you considered the price of even the most fucked up roach infested 20 jeet shared room lately?
>Back in my days 1 latte paid for the mortgage. Kids these days...

62386231
>>62382627
Yep I'm a grade IV with only an associates. I just got my restricted before applying for jobs with zero experience and within 4 years I'm making 34$/hr.

62386247
>>62386120
Not every job is actually worth your labor. Keep your self respect up bro, you’ll make it

62386262
>>62382527
USA Is a country for losers. Get out and find a better life.

62386267
Safe index.

62386343
I'm ugly because of shit genetics so I should be paid a disability check it's literally the same thing as having a disability.

It has negatively affected my life to where it has prevented me from achieving success therefore I should be subsidized.

Why do people with autism get disability, and btw I have both but I never claimed autism was my downfall. Being ugly is 10x worse you can't even work a menial job because zoomies will make fun of you

62386365
>>62386343
bullshit Im 10x uglier and command respect and always make friends and partnerships because I act chadlike. yur issue is not your face its in your fucking brain.

62386369
>>62386365
Nta but there is a point of ugliness that society is not cool with regardless of attitude.

62386425
>>62384161
Imagine how easy you would end up employed if you were a woman

62386498
>>62382527

Viewpoint214.com

is for you and anyone else who needs help...

All you have to do is enter your name and email address.

No scams or ponzi schemes. 100% legit.

62386569
>>62386343

>Downfall

It's downSIDE you fucking retard. Get a job, invest, and stop gambling on retard-tier stocks. 20k in NLCP would get you $2400/year for sitting on your ass. Well, maybe 2200 now, idgaf I bought when it was yielding 15% and I've gotten banger dividends with mild capital appreciation and almost zero risk.

Job = living expenses. Invest in a few solid companies. Don't buy hype bullshit. Don't by hot sectors. By good companies in sectors nobody gives a shit about, like Cannabis, Agriculture, REITs, etc. Don't buy any company unless it has rock-solid financials.

And stop saying downfalls you fucking faggot this isn't an Anime.

62386574
>>62386425
Am i too ugly to be employed as a woman? Can’t find a job either

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 75 replies — 39.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-18 23:53 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377880
7. We scour /smg/ as per >>62374886 and report back to this thread with the underrated, most talked about and genuinely good picks. Since there are multiple /smg/ threads this would be great.

NOTE: We should just use the current /smg/ threads and the archived ones at least within the past 1-2 years. So if you know a stock that everyone is talking about on reddit, please die. Reddit is not /smg/. Our pool that we search through will be /smg/. It would be best as it would help narrow our search and some people on /smg/ really do know their stuff. There is an individual there bragging daily about how he told everyone to buy Intel.

I am open to criticism and suggestions

62377886
>>62373752
If you actually want to get out of five figures your best bet is a six figure salary. I'm not joking, invest in yourselves.

62377896
if you have earned income and don’t have a retirement account, I would open a Roth IRA in addition to a regular taxable investment account. get in the habit of making regular contributions to both accounts. automatic deposits would be best if possible. even if you don’t make a lot, getting in the habit of investing is worthwhile. it’s best to invest in an etf like VTI or an s&p 500 etf. if there are dividends, set up automatic dividend reinvestment. (If you have a taxable account you’ll still need to pay tax on dividends, even if you reinvest them).

62378002
>>62373918
Well the /ic/ thread got deleted within minutes this is why I am cautious whenever we start to plan or congregate together its like they hate that.

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

62380691
Bump

62381251
Bump

62381343
>>62378002
/ic/ is filled with schizoids and endless seethe. And this is coming from someone who draws as a hobby.

62381797
>>62373752
>4 figure hell
unironically get a job, any job
like just work a year lol

62383107
It's settled, tune in tomorrow!

62383529
>>62373752
I am all in on SpaceX with my poorstack €6k

62383569
>>62373752
finally,a good general

62384962
Yes, leverage everything and bet it all on the moon shot. You won't regret it.

62384983
Patiently waiting for the hundred bucks of link I bought a decade ago to turn me into a millionaire.

Any day now.

62384990
>>62381797
I AM TRYING
BUT NOBODY IS HIRING

62384995
>>62378002
It's base policy to stop /ic/ from turning into a Patreon beggar spam board

62385082
>>62384990
Use your CPU to mine Nerva/XNV then. A properly decentralized crypto with no mining pools. Even a laptop works!

62385259
I bought 3 shares of spacex at 172 each should I kill myself

62385391
>>62381343
Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
>>62384995
Yeah that makes sense, although they do have a dedicated thread to shill their blogs which would conveniently link up to any Patreon.

62385458
>>62385259
why are you chasing hype

62385552
>>62385458
not smart

62385679
>>62373888
Well video games suck ass now. But we have AI which is supposed to help make projects like that easier. Godot is a free open source video game engine that is supposedly easy to use. Why don’t we learn how to do that?

62385716
>>62385391
>Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
Sure

62385756
>>62385716
https://archive.4plebs.org/pol/thread/528268175/#528271334

Two links are in the archive one is for shitposting and the other is more art related it's the only way to share them here.

62385759
>>62383107
Ok, so you said it’s settled. What is the decision? Btw if your current mascot doesnt work out I nominate that we use anime Elizabeth Holmes as our mascot. Plus she better exemplifies us anyways.

62385818
34 with 8k to my name
Should I just give up? Honest question

62386248
I have a negative NW of $45k at 31. Unemployed. I don’t know how it all went so wrong for me. I don’t know how to fix it. I have a degree but it doesn’t do shit for me. Worst part is that I had almost $300k 4 years ago lmao.

62386520
>>62386248
How did that happen

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 77 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE…

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-06-18 21:00 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

62369133
>>62368990
>>62368990
You can make friends with women too anon. Women that you aren't interested in can have friends that you are interested in.

Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.

Unironically truely befriend a lesbian, this will teach you how to act around women without acting like a retard as you won't try to fuck her.

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62369150
All the matches I got on tinder is 30s post wall roast, I can’t believe the 28yi tinder matches something literally call me old hag when I’m just a 35yo bald, obese, needy, short, no STEM degree, and potentially impotent.

62369807
>>62365540
Elon is worth $1,000,000,000,000 and still can't find a woman that wants to stay with him

62371591
>>62369133
> take shrooms
Based

>>62368990
You’re letting your insecurities delude you.

62371912
>>62365555
you sound like a retard

62371972
>>62369133
>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
holy bluepilled retard

62373795
>>62369807
tbf net worth is not how much money you have

62374837
>>62371972
You're correct that having boundaries isn't insecurity, but not trying to ask your friends if they know a single girl because you believe she talked to your friend is deeply insecure. No one here was talking about behavior after meeting, this dude won't even want to speak to a girl that would've known a male friend
NGMI behavior

62378030
>>62365555
What is up with normies and "cooking"??? I like preparing my own fucking meals and I don't want to cook with you nor eat your food nor make some big show about THE DINNER TABLE NARF NARF NARF like food should be strictly for utilitarian purposes only. Also women have a sinful relationship with food. You would think it was porn to them. Foodies and fat people gross me out. Humans have to ruin so many things. Don't even get me started on BLACK PEOPLE

62378038
>>62365608
Mainly non brown chicks right?

62378415
>>62365540
You have been priced out of prime young teen. Start dating older woman which is closer to the post wall in youth.

62378662
>>62365555
>got a blu ray player
Why not just get on PTP instead of wasting your money?
>>62365595
How old were you?
>>62365989
Holy fuck. I will remain khhv forever
>>62369226
Depends on age and many other factors
>>62369807
He had a threesome with Cara Delivigne and Amber heard, got prime Amber to do a high quality cosplay and roles of his waifu (mercy) for him, and made prime alt princess grimes completely obsessed with him. He won multiple times over when it comes to women, especially compared to other billionaires.

62378677
>>62378662
>How old were you?
32

62378686
>>62378677
>32
So i still have time, like 2 years away. $800k net. I'm pretty sure I'll hit 1m by then but not so sure about the gf.

62378720
>>62378686
you just launched a whole interdimensional TED Talk powered by three screenshots, two podcasts, and a gas-station energy drink.
You're standing on a folding chair yelling, "EVERY SINGLE WOMAN CAN JUST BECOME RICH, DATE A CELEBRITY, FIND A SUGAR DADDY, OR MARRY SOME DEVOTED GUY," like life is a character-select screen in a video game where everybody spawns with max charisma and infinite social options.
My brother in chaos, that's not how reality works.
You took a handful of visible examples, put them in a blender, added internet rage, and came out with a theory that somehow explains half the human population. That's clown astronomy. You're looking at three bright stars and declaring you've mapped the entire galaxy.
And then when someone tells a story about a rude interaction on a dating app, your brain teleports straight back to the same speech. Like the conversation took a bus stop and you arrived riding a shopping cart with no wheels.
The wild part is that your argument assumes every person has equal opportunities, equal looks, equal luck, equal social skills, equal location, equal connections, and equal willingness to do the things you're listing. That's a level of simplification so aggressive it could flatten a mountain into a parking lot.
Reality is messy. Some people struggle. Some people succeed. Some get lucky. Some don't. Some are attractive and still miserable. Some are average and find great relationships. Human lives aren't assembled from four preset buttons.
So when you scream "EVERY SINGLE ONE," that's the clown horn giving itself CPR.
The issue isn't that you're making a point. The issue is that you're treating a giant, complicated reality like a meme template and acting surprised when people look confused.
You're not describing the world.
You're describing a cartoon version of the world that lives rent-free inside your own echo chamber.

62378730
>>62378720
Ok chatgpt very cool

62378739
>>62366590
Those families are pre 2025+ hypergamy

62378802
>>62366052
>he thinks the average foid has less rates of herpes
lol
lmao

At least high class escorts routinely get checked. You think the average woman's ego would allow her to get regular checks? We're literally at the stage where high class, well-regulated services are safer than your typical bar hookup.

62379944
>>62365621
can you explain to me what a "pickme" is?
I've seen youtube shorts but I still don't understand because it's girls who wear hats and shorts and go to sports bars.
>>62365555
>nothing about sexo
that's a friend not a girlfriend.

62379954
>>62365555
What your looking for is a boyfriend. Straight men do drugs jack off and play video games

62379965
>>62365621
Same man. I've never had a real conversation with a girl save one cool chick in college I dated.

They don't like deep intellectual discussions or pondering the nature of how something works

62379978
>>62379944
>wow I totally love video games
>omg I'm a huge Warhammer 40k fan
Look inside: regular chick with passing interest in the stuff you like
Pick me is pretty self explanatory, they want to be 'picked.
If anything sports chicks are more likely to turn on a game and want to watch in my experience but sports are kinda gay

62380391
>>62365555
Yeah sorry buddy, you need to be 20 in the early 2000s for that life, now it is only fans and thotting around for the next 50 years. Women are more susceptible to the psychic social media poison

62380855
>>62379978
weird but I think I've met a few. they always seemed fake

62382230
>>62365540
Being broke works the best for me personally

62385537
>>62365626
Ironic that Henry looks better than you, but came to the conclusion life is better settling with a basic girl and staying at home playing video games

62385567
>>62365748
this. Even if you marry a gold digger, it's not fulfilling. It's why Bill Gates etc just stick to 10/10 escorts ordered right to their hotels discretely and the occasional island trips.

>>62369807
see above. Obviously he can, but there's no point in legally marrying someone who's poorer than you. He's also too degen. He was literally trying to bring his ex wife to the Epstein island thing with him when they were still together. I think he can't relate to any girls unless they're really fucked in the head like Grimes and even she couldn't stand his work schedule after a couple years.

>>62378415
yeah, if not attractive enough to fuck/marry them in middle/high school, it's over. Either settle with someone nice and helpful or/and rent chicks out for the hour.

62385595
>>62365540
Invest in my startup. Bitches love investors.

62385960
>>62380855
Because they are, they’re putting on a front to be picked instead of being themselves. Like that red headed pearl lady

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 54 replies — 15.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-18 22:12 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372037
>>62371763
i see, thanks for the input

62372074
>>62371737
Heyyy we're port buddies :) YAGMI

62372118
>>62371737
also this fails to recognize buying more shares bringing that rate down significantly

62372136
>>62372074
>>62371737
I lied my port's now at 77k LOL YANGMI

62372167
Due to volatility and human nature its actually mentally easier to go into 5 companies you believe in. keep most money in S&P and add 5

62372242
>>62372136
errrrm ok buddy

62373602
Do we got a chance for 100k by years end fellas

62373605
>hey guys daddy got me a job making 6 figs, please tell me how to be a millionaire
Nepobabies really think life is just people giving them all the tips to succeed, for free, huh. Lol.

62373753
>>62373602
Im all in on nasdaq but yes

62374176
mmmm sweaty office boobies mmm yes
asphyxiated to death by her big milkies mmmm
yes

62374196
>>62370048
>I miss the touch of a girl
shut the frick up fakecel

62374412
>>62373753
What in particular are you in

62374944
>>62373602
Tbh I want to raise the amount of principal I’m putting into the account but have zero ideas for side money to put into it

62377984
>>62374944
bumpin this idea

62377988
>>62370048
Love is gay.

62377998
>>62369835
Triples and I will chop my cock off

62378015
>>62377998
holy fuck

62378019
>>62374412
NDQ on the asx

62378395
>>62377998
Circumcise it , you got close enough

62379025
>>62378356
errrrrm middle class bro?

62379549
Going over this one last time, Nasdaq has been eating S&P’s lunch, but only because mega cap tech stocks have had a great decade
Additionally, it isn’t that VOO has had bad returns, since it crosses over into a lot of QQQM, just that QQQM is more volatile
So basically it’s a choice on whether you believe the companies doing good will continue to do good with the current market and AI trends, and how volatile you’re allowing your portfolio to be

62379580
>>62371737
Brother, I’m just an anon on this dead basket weaving forum, but this isn’t 1970 or 2010 or even 2020. Get the fuck out of VOO.

62379597
>>62379580
>what do you suggest then?
Physical gold/silver, guns, bullets, tools, food with long shelf life, eth/link in September

62379618
>>62379580
>>62379597
This is the equivalent of Ketotards on /fit/

62382033
>>62379618
lol

62384221
>>62369835
Bumping cause I’m still unsure if I pair VOO with QQQ and just have a ton of overlap and be weighted towards market cap or just do a sector ETF like SOXX or just all VOO (all QQQ seems dumb to me)

62384784
>>62370048
Reading this just about blackpilled me. No matter how much I convince myself I'm special, some of you retards cooks up the exact same train of thought.

62384838
>>62384221
Have you considered tracking a global index like the MSCI ACWI? Some are predicting the World outperforming the US for the next while. But I have two balls and neither is crystal.

62386154
>>62384838
no but it's a possibility now that you mentioned it, for now the goal is 100k or more in voo

62386193
>>62384784
We're all humans, anon. See the positive side, maybe some Roman centurion had these same insecurities about women back in the past and remained a virgin incel his whole life, maybe some middle age scholar had the same existential dread people experience today but no distractions from the echo of the cave other than reading books and knowing in his heart that pursuing knowledge was also a vanity of vanities

item.title: How is your career going? — 64 replies — 12.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-06-18 20:46 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382822
>>62381049
Have you identified which government regulations secure your job?
Seems like an important thing to know.

62382877
>>62381049
27m, 150k, fully remote. on track to become senior and make ~180k

swe

62382899
>>62381049
24m
$45/hr as a Information Management/Technical Consultant.
Been at it for 4 years:
-Mostly just showing people how to organize data and operate basic proprietary corpo programs.
-90% of the work is physical (walking in, seeing something has obviously been done wrong, fixing it)
Job security is that people will always have trouble with the machines they use on a day to day basis. People will always be stupid and mess things up.

>>62382184
On the equipment side, stenotype's have been around since the 1910's so you can easily get comfortable with the layout on a typewriter from the 1950's for $30.

>>62382303
>Weekly hours?
It's rural, my usual is roughly 30 hours. I get calls from 100+ miles away who are willing to pay enough to have me come out and teach.
A lot of the extra dough comes from just claiming what the company wants to through out tech wise, then wiping and reselling their entire server (made in 2021) online or in person through third party marketplaces. Make about double what the hourly is with that.

>>62382530
>how did you get started
I joke to a lot of the upper class clients "no one hired me, so I hired myself"

>>62382877
Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

62382947
>>62382899
>Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

we have some foreign contractors but we have a shit ton of funding covering burn so we can afford to hire citizens. it's not a unique business but we're niche in our approach and field (data/ai). we're actually hiring right now.

62383011
33m 110k a year working in crypto, remote.
Workplace changed from based and nice to kinda idiotic thanks to poor business decisions, AI brain rot, and limited runway.

I'm trying to change but everything else pays like shit or is a cult. IT makes me want to kill myself at this point. I have golden handcuffs

I just want to find a way to accumulate so much capital that I can live off dividends.

62383016
32k CAD ($20.25/hr) after being at WMT for 10 years
I know it's not the best job, but it's low effort, my coworkers and bosses and customers love me, and I can keep working sustainably. I've managed to get about 140k CAD net worth, too.

62383085
>>62382530
Basically I got hired from a craigslist posting for a middleman tech company ran by some bulgarian. He would send us out to clients and pay us less than half, scalping the rest. I have a legitimate interest in tech and build things with hardware, networks and software so some of these places I helped set up and was friendly helpful to them. It also let me learn the missing parts of each field and then I was able to piece it all together. Since they were the clients, I would interact with the CEOs, Owners and top end leadership of each company. You vibe well with them and not be inappropriate (hitting on, trying to get numbers, be a creep) they will want you, even do power plays to get you. I quit the middleman cause he would send me to do shit that gets paid 170k salary (85/hr) and pay me 21/hr and scalp the other 64, while not helping me really. I was able to chill for a year, then i reached out to one of these clients and they picked me right up. Since they wanted me, I had the leverage to negotiate a higher salary, which they had no problem paying. They liked me so much they just have me run the whole IT department, I dont even really have oversight. I do have to know my shit, but AI makes it 1000x easier and if you arent using AI you arent gonna make it. Literally complete tasks that would take months to do in days. These guys can't even fathom that kind of efficiency so they think im busy all the time. AI is literally a cheat code, especially if you know your shit and use it to do that shit. I'm close to 6 figs starting, will easily hit 6 figs after solve some of their money loss headaches (which i have done for a major part).
>Getting interest from potential employers gives you huge leverage, esp if they know you are a game changer
The best part is I asked if I could start my own projects for learning, that they would benefit the company and the CEO is hyped for it. I am literally getting paid to self improve

62383096
>>62381049
I'm a warehouse grunt
$20 / hour
How do I even go up from here? Maybe a forklift license?

62383304
I make 31 an hour doing quality assurance for asphalt and I think about killing myself every day.

62383355
>>62381049
I make $29/hour driving the city bus.
I have a class B CDL.
While the pay is nothing special, the benefits are incredible, the hours are great, and the company overall is a dream to work for.
I plan on leeching every last cent I can out of this place before getting a fat retirement in 30 years.

62383464
>>62382153
It won't, everything has yearly inspections

62383470
Oh yeah its going fucking awful. I make $23/hr annoying people at their homes. I fully expect to be killed one day doing this but someone's gotta do it

62383481
$48 per hour? Thats $2000 per week. You can buy otm SPY contracts and double your weekly net. The issue is. Nobody want to work learn a life changing skill outside of their job because theyre so mentally exhausted from everyday distraction & life. We call ourselves the 1%'ers

62383500
>>62381049
late 20's, 120K in business analytics. It's an adult daycare but the pay's good, feels bad going in the office once a week tho. There are some qts around but that's just about all my social interaction for the week. My dream is to keep the same amount of pay but work in another country in another time zone, but that would be rough. Seriously thinking about it one day

62383513
i make $130k base + 15% bonus target as a corporate accounting manager. i'm underpaid by like $20k/yr but i'm fully remote so i accept it. kind of wish i had done law school instead.

62383520
>>62381049
Just bombed a senior product manager and coordinator interview.
I talk too much and don’t get to the point. That’s the direct feedback I got, which is true.

I have another offer I already accepted at a faang company though. It’s lower level and need to move though.

62383530
>>62383520
>It’s lower level and need to move though.
are you going to do it? i can't imagine moving for work in the year 2026, when these big indianjewnigger companies can (and do) blindly lay people off all the time.

62383536
>>62381049
100% stuck at a job I hate that boring as fuck most days and the few days where I actually need to work I bust my ass off all day because my retarded managers will fuck the schedule up and have only one person on to do three peoples worth of work. I lucked into a middle management position though in a field with basically no mobility so I can't really leave without taking a huge paycut. Make $36/hr which feels like nothing because I also live in a pretty expensive area.

62383538
>>62383096
Whatever you want anon. Start a business on the side or smthg.
>>62383530
I will because can’t take the current company anymore or I will Elliott Rodger someone in Minecraft.

62383574
>>62382822
Commerical and residential buildings in my state literally cannot be occupied if they don't pass a fire inspection

62383585
I deliver mail for 30 bucks an hour. Paid for 8.5 hours usually done in 5-6, it's a weird evaluated quazi salaried thing.

62384021
>>62381049
Early 40s, trapped in a flyover shithole making $80k in data analytics. Entry level title with a senior-level workload and enough experience to at least land interviews for data engineering roles with some regularity, though there are only a handful of openings in my area each year. I'm pretty frugal and on track to be able to semi-retire in Asia within a few years which is looking increasingly likely given the state of the job market and my career.

62384045
i'm a family doc and make ~130/hr. it sucks but at least my schedule is condensed into 4 days a week and i'm hoping to retire in 9 years.

62384057
>42
>middle manager but my job is going to be outsourced in the next year or so
>110k before taxes
>live in New York suburbs so it’s actually not as much money
I have a lot invested and saved so when I’m axed it’s tempting to just try to survive on that instead of working again. no family to support and I inherited the house I live in with no mortgage.

62384184
>$37/hour salary equivalent post taxes and $1k a month 401k contribution
is this good

62384198
>>62384184
Depends on CoL in your area, but it's better than most.

62384480
$50k cad customer support
36 yrs old
I am severely underperforming but I love making music -- married a woman from a well off family, we have a house
Confidence low, bros, confidence low

62384785
>>62384480
As it should be, you're the woman in the relationship.

62385873
>>62384785
Not exactly, but maybe to an extent as I am an artistic man. I'm definitely the defender though.

Not sure how to upgrade but also keep up with my artistry which I feel is my greatest passion and inspiration

At the very least I feel teaching your children music and related science opens up their mind to a lot of thinking

62385905
I make sandwitch

32 per hour aud 48 hr weeks

item.title: You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor… — 37 replies — 12.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-18 22:49 UTC

OP

You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mortgage. You don't need plane tickets when a road trip can save money. You don't need all those subscriptions. You don't need to hire landscapers. You don't need apple products. You don't need student loans. You don't need a smart fridge. You don't need fancy food for your dog. You don't need a 4k tv. You don't need to go skiing. You don't need a boat. You don't need to eat at restaurants. You don't need a gaming PC. You don't need to pay more than $30 for a haircut. Or anything really. You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco. You don't need to shop at Best Buy. You don't need to buy a bunch of fancy ingredients and larp as a chef. You don't need a granite counter top. You don't need to buy Claude tokens. You don't need to buy sports tickets. You don't need a cat. You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill. You don't need to pay for new tires. You can get them used. You don't need to buy premium gas. You don't need to tell the insurance company how many people live with you. You don't need to buy any books, use project Gutenberg. You don't need all those tools in the garage. You don't use half of them anyways. You don't need a home with a garage. You don't need to be on the HOA. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your gf. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your wife. You don't need to buy condoms. You don't need to buy diapers. You don't need all sorts of sauces and condiments in the fridge. You don't need warranties ANY warranty, just take care of your things. You don't need to go to concerts. You don't need to have a blue checkmark. You don't need to eat food at events. You don't need a house with stairs. You don't need carpet on those stairs. You don't need a leather couch. You don't need to have the fastest wifi. You don't need to buy pornographic art. You don't need to buy beer. Or any drug.

Early replies (first 3)

62377958
>>62377955
man no one is reading allat nigga fucking die in a fire.

62378430
some wealthy people have so much money they don't even have enough time to spend it. the costs of things is just another thing that takes time. the secret is to remove the things that gatekeep time so the time can be spent on things you want not have to

62378534
>>62377955
I don't know what you meant by this but this was the most relaxing thing I've ever read and it describes my life and outlook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378617
>>62377955
I'm not reading that wall of text.

I already have a new car (Toyota Corolla) paid off. My rent is $900/mo and I split it with my mom, so it's really only $450. I don't like sitting in a car for hours or driving on a freeway. A flight to Miami from where I live is about 2.5 hours, a car ride would be 19 hours if you don't stop for food, restroom, gas or sleep.

62378622
>>62378617
no one asked

62378624
>>62377955
I do want some of these things however, and I will use money to buy them. What, am I supposed to live miserably like some FIRE redditor and spend the best years of my life physically on wage slaving for a future that may never come or that I will be too damaged to enjoy? No thanks.

62378680
>>62378622
ESL?

Learn proper grammar, retard.

62378689
>>62378680
you might want to go back to school if you think that this isn't proper grammar, nigger

62378695
>>62377955
I need the cute and funny tho.

62378857
based op

62378869
>>62378689
>He still doesn't capitalize the first letter, or end a comment with a period.

62378877
>>62378869
this has nothing to do with proper grammar or ESL. Nobody types like this any longer, fucking boomer

62378880
>>62378877
Don't text me until you're educated enough to have an intellectual conversation.

62378883
>>62378880
take the L, grandpa

62378886
>>62378883
*Accept you have lost, senior citizen.

62378888
>>62378886
bro, just say you have asperger, you already gave it away the moment you said you live with your mom

62378897
>>62378888
Quads don't lie...

62378926
>>62377958
why do you speak like a little nigger from the jungle ?

62379963
>>62377955
tell that to the communist invaders who are actually materialists

62379983
>>62377955
>You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco.
but those places save you money...

62380058
yeah, and commies should get shot at the head and get hanged on electric poles with their guts out

62380088
>>62377955
I need all of that + more, so fuck you and fork it over porky *shoves gun in your face*

62380106
>>62377955
>You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill
>$35
thats way too much. 700pc overpriced. mint mobile is $5/mo.
get on my level.

62380264
>>62380106
>Mint mobile - $15/mo
Get on my level, Tello mobile.
$10/ account .
I already rolled over 3 numbers there that I don't need. And have 1 extra backup phone and phone line.

62382946
>>62380264
>>62380106
ENTER
https://www.ebay.com/itm/136840233242

62382962
>>62377955
You are right OP. Every single company providing these goods and services should go broke from loss of revenue. All wagies need to lose their jobs.

62382977
>>62377955
I need my 2k a month mortgage or i will lose my house. Also, i'm not getting rid of my cats.

62383138
Also, i'm not getting rid of my cats

62384604
>>62377955
Costco is excellent THO

62384613
Also Sam's Club saves you money in the long run, buying bulk is the way of the future .

62384686
Also ITT Millionaires on paper teaching the kids how to live like the -30K in debt immigrant family down the street so they can put .001% more dividends into meme memory blue chips

62384696
>>62377955
wtf is this ai slop

62386324
>>62377955
You don't need a life. You can just kill yourself.
